---
title: "4.11 2D Array Intro and Storage"
subtitle: "2D Array Creation and Access"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## 2D Array Creation and Access

### Rows and columns

Arrays in Java can store many items of the same type.

You can even store items in **two-dimensional** (2D) arrays which are arrays that have both **rows** and **columns**.

A **row** has horizontal elements.

A **column** has vertical elements.

## Lockers in Rows and Columns {.image-fit}

![Lockers in rows and columns](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-11-2d-array-creation-and-access/2DLockers.jpg)

Figure 1: Lockers in rows and columns.

In the picture there are 3 rows of lockers and 6 columns.

## Natural 2D Data

### Data organized in rows and columns

Two dimensional arrays are especially useful when the data is naturally organized in rows and columns like:

- a spreadsheet
- bingo
- battleship
- theater seats
- classroom seats
- a picture

In battleship, letters map to the rows and the column indices start with `1`.

## Array of Arrays

### Java storage model

A **2D array** is stored as an array of arrays in Java.

The way 2D arrays are created and indexed is similar to 1D array objects.

The size of a 2D array is established at the time of creation and cannot be changed.

2D arrays can store either primitive data or object reference data.

## Array Storage

### Row-major and column-major order

Many programming languages store two-dimensional array data in a one-dimensional array.

**Row-major** order stores all the data for the first row followed by all the data for the second row and so on.

**Column-major** order stores all the data for the first column followed by all the data for the second column and so on.

## Row-Major and Column-Major {.image-fit}

![A 2D array stored in row-major order or column-major order as a 1D array](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-11-2d-array-creation-and-access/rowMajor.png)

Figure 2: A 2D array stored in row-major order or column-major order as a 1D array.

## Java Arrays of Arrays {.image-fit}

![Java arrays of arrays](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-11-2d-array-creation-and-access/ArrayRowsAndCols.png)

Figure 3: Java arrays of arrays.

Java stores a two-dimensional array as an array of arrays.

Each element of the outer array has a reference to each inner array.

## Exam Assumption

### Rectangular row-major arrays

On the exam assume that any 2 dimensional (2D) array is in row-major order.

The outer array can be thought of as the rows and the inner arrays the columns.

On the exam all inner arrays will have the same length.

It is possible in Java to have inner arrays of different lengths, also called **non-rectangular arrays** or **ragged arrays**, but they are outside the AP CSA exam scope.

## Check Your Understanding

### Clickable area converted to classroom task

Use this table for the next questions.

|     | c0 | c1 | c2 | c3 |
| --- | --: | --: | --: | --: |
| r0 | 8 | -2 | 3 | -1 |
| r1 | 4 | 5 | 0 | -7 |
| r2 | 2 | -3 | -4 | -5 |

Rows are horizontal and columns are vertical. Both start with index `0`.

## Trace Check

### `clickablearea:: clicktd1` and `clicktd2`

1. Click on all the values in the row at index `2`.

Correct values:

```text
2, -3, -4, -5
```

2. Click on all the values in the column at index `1`.

Correct values:

```text
-2, 5, -3
```

## Trace Check

### `clickablearea:: clicktd3`, `clicktd4`, `clicktd5`

1. Click on the value at row index `2` and column index `1`.

Correct value: `-3`

2. Click on the value at row index `0` and column index `2`.

Correct value: `3`

3. Click on the value at row index `1` and column index `1`.

Correct value: `5`

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

- define a 2D array as an array with rows and columns
- identify row as horizontal and column as vertical
- explain Java stores a 2D array as an array of arrays
- use row-major order for AP CSA exam reasoning
- use `row index` first and `column index` second

## End

### 4.11 2D Array Intro and Storage

Next: Declaring 2D arrays and initializer lists.
